title: phalaroides arundinacea subsp. oehleri
intro: Phalaroides arundinacea subsp. oehleri, commonly known as Phalaris arundinacea subsp. oehleri, is a sub-species of the common reed grass, a type of perennial grass native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Phalaris arundinacea subsp. oehleri include treating digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and immune function.
